Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 073. On Aššur-bessunu[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na] ⸢LUGAL⸣ EN-ia

(1) [To the kin]g, my lord: [your servan]t Ṭab-šar-Aššur. [Good health] to the king, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD]-⸢ka⸣ mDÙG.GA—IM—aš-šur

o 33

[lu DI-mu] ⸢a⸣-na LUGAL be-lí-ia

o 44

[ina UGU maš]-⸢šur⸣—bé-⸢sún⸣

(4) [As to Ašš]ur-bessunu [the ... of whom] the king, my lord, [wrote to m]e: "I have given him [...] oxen [......

o 55

[x x x ša] LUGAL be-lí

o 66

[iš-pur-an]-⸢ni⸣ ma-a ⸢GUD?⸣-MEŠ

o 77

[x x x x x] ⸢a⸣-ta-na-šú

o 88

[x x x x x x] ⸢URU?.ku⸣-lu-man

o 99

[x x x x x x]+⸢x⸣ x [x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr beginning destroyed, then 7 uninscribed lines

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P336597/.
